claim: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Analyzed the local file os_implementable_improvement_plan_full_memory_v1 (1).md in detail with 12 subagents. Verified file SHA-256 4B8C7447D4048781935EC7D8B8223112953941BEB4060AA0661AD6D298AE4DE7, size 115,429 bytes, 1,285 lines. All agents treated the document as a strong final-readiness/specification plan rather than an already accepted/completed OS proof. Key retained knowledge: final OS must be local-first, GitHub-backed, Codex/Claude peer memory authority, graph-visible, evidence-regenerating, Korean user-visible, and must reduce repeated explanation through grounded memory use. Consensus risks: same document SHA is insufficient without a frozen memory snapshot/manifest SHA; P1-10 retrieval contract is too thin; P1-16 task lifecycle/finish grounding should move earlier; P2-17 outcome metrics should become a global readiness gate; P0-04 tool parity needs schema/config/data-root proof, not just tool names; P1-11 Korean DOM/screenshot proof is a global dependency; first implementation slice should be P0-02 status freshness only.
